zh.lifeSummary: 當Claricia De Hartley於1223年出生在英國戴文哈特雷時，她的父親Adam De Hartley36歲，母親Cecilia31歲。她和Sir John de la Lynde至少有2個兒子。她去世於1283年之後在英國素可波萊布盧可。

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Hartley
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Lancashire and Yorkshire): habitational name, in northern England mainly from Hartley in Rochdale parish (Lancashire) but also from any of the places called Hartley in Westmorland and the West Yorkshire. In southern England it derives Hartley in Devon, Hampshire, and Kent, and from Hartleigh in Devon. Similar placenames occur in Berkshire, Dorset, and Northumberland, but it is not known if they gave rise to surnames. Most of the placenames derive from Old English heorot ‘hart, stag’ + lēah ‘woodland clearing’, though the Westmorland placename comes from Old English heard ‘hard’ + clā ‘claw, tongue of land’, and the Northumberland placename derives from Old English heorot + hlāw ‘mound, hill’.
Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó hArtghaile ‘descendant of Artghal’, a personal name composed of the elements Art ‘bear’ or ‘hero’ + gal ‘valor’.

variants.name: Artley
meanings: English: variant of Hartley with loss of initial H-.

variants.name: Hartless
meanings: English (Staffordshire): variant of Hartley or of Hartle , with post-medieval excrescent -s. The spelling of the modern surname is no doubt due to confusion with the English vocabulary word heartless, meaning ‘cruel or unfeeling’.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

en.lifeSummary: When Claricia De Hartley was born in 1223, in Hartley, Devon, England, her father, Adam De Hartley, was 36 and her mother, Cecilia, was 31. She had at least 2 sons with Sir John de la Lynde. She died after 1283, in Bolebrook, Sussex, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Claricia De Hartleyが1223年にイギリスデボンハートリーで生まれた時，父Adam De Hartleyは36歳，母Ceciliaは31歳であった。彼女はSir John de la Lyndeとの間に少なくとも2人の息子をもうけた。彼女は1283年以後にイギリススセックスボレブルークで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Claricia De Hartley(이)가 1223년 영국 데폰 하틀리에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Adam De Hartley(은)는 36세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Cecilia(은)는 31세였다. 그녀는 Sir John de la Lynde(와)과 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1283년 이후 영국 수세스 볼레브룩에서 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Claricia De Hartley родилась в 1223 г. в местности хартли, девон, Англия, её отцу по имени Adam De Hartley было 36 лет, а матери, которую звали Cecilia, 31.
